products.name: Dada Ji's Laal Maas
products.story: Born from Dada Ji's 4 AM roasting ritual. Every morning since 1897, he hand-roasts mathania chilies and cumin on his ancestral cast-iron kadhai. This masala has made grown men cry — and come back for seconds. The secret? A precise 23-minute slow roast that no machine can replicate.
products.usage: Perfect for authentic Laal Maas, any red meat curry, or as a fiery finishing sprinkle on kebabs.

products.name: Rani Sa's Dal Baati
products.story: Rani Sa learned this blend in the royal kitchens of Udaipur, where her grandmother was head cook for the Maharana's family. The 'aroma test' is non-negotiable — if it doesn't make neighbours knock asking 'kya ban raha hai?', it's not ready. A secret pinch of saffron makes this unforgettable.
products.usage: Essential for Dal Baati Churma, panchmel dal, or any Rajasthani lentil preparation.

products.name: Maharaja Sa's Gatte
products.story: Maharaja Sa doesn't cook — he commands. With a single sniff, he can tell you the exact village where the cumin was harvested. This masala is blended with mathematical precision — 23 spices, each measured to the milligram on a brass scale from 1842. He once rejected a batch because the coriander was 'harvested two days too late.'
products.usage: Made for Gatte ki Sabzi, also works beautifully in kadhi, besan-based snacks, and pakoras.

products.name: Dadi Sa's Ker Sangri
products.story: Dadi Sa walks barefoot through the Thar Desert at dawn, collecting wild ker berries and sangri pods that only she can find. Her recipes use ingredients from no cookbook — desert flowers, wild seeds, and a mysterious 'raat ki mitti' that gives her masala its earthy depth. Nobody argues with Dadi Sa.
products.usage: The only masala for authentic Ker Sangri. Also perfect for Papad ki Sabzi and desert-style pickles.

products.name: Kunwar Sa's Safed Maas
products.story: The prince's masala — delicate, refined, and devastatingly aromatic. Kunwar Sa created this blend for the white meat curry that was served only at royal celebrations. Cashew, poppy seeds, and white pepper create a creamy, mild heat that whispers rather than shouts.
products.usage: For Safed Maas (white meat curry), creamy chicken korma, or any mild, aromatic gravy.

products.name: Thakur Sa's Mohan Maas
products.story: Thakur Sa's masterpiece — the 'enchanting meat' masala that combines sweet and spicy in perfect harmony. Legend says this recipe was created to impress a visiting dignitary who couldn't handle heat. The result? A masala so balanced, it enchants everyone who tastes it.
products.usage: Exclusively for Mohan Maas — the sweet-spiced royal meat curry. Also works in rich biryanis.

products.name: Rawat Sa's Jungli Maas
products.story: The hunter's masala — raw, primal, and unapologetically fierce. Rawat Sa created this for the 'wild meat' cooked over open fires during hunting expeditions. Just three core spices, but in proportions that create an explosion. Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication.
products.usage: For Jungli Maas — the minimalist, fiery game meat curry. Also great for BBQ rubs and tandoor marinades.

products.name: Chhote Sarkar's Mirchi Vada
products.story: The youngest in the family, Chhote Sarkar rules the streets. His Mirchi Vada masala captures the exact flavour of Jodhpur's famous street stalls — the ones with hour-long queues. He spent 3 years perfecting the potato filling spice ratio. The result? Street food royalty.
products.usage: For Mirchi Vada filling, aloo tikki, samosa filling, or any street-style potato preparation.

products.name: Bua Sa's Chai Masala
products.story: Bua Sa's chai is legendary in the family — nobody leaves her house without at least 3 cups. Her secret? A masala ground fresh every Monday morning with proportions she's never written down. 'Chai mein pyaar hona chahiye' (chai needs love), she says, adding an extra pinch of cardamom.
products.usage: Add 1/4 tsp per cup while boiling chai. Also wonderful in desserts, smoothies, and baked goods.

products.name: Mama Sa's Achaar Masala
products.story: Mama Sa's pickle masala is the reason every jar in the family disappears within a week. His secret technique: sun-drying each spice for exactly 3 days before grinding. 'Achaar mein shortcut nahi chalega' (no shortcuts in pickle), he insists. The tanginess is perfectly balanced.
products.usage: For homemade pickles (mango, lemon, mixed), or sprinkle on raita and salads for instant tang.

products.name: Nani Sa's Papad Masala
products.story: Nani Sa can turn a plain papad into a feast. Her masala transforms the humble papad sabzi into something guests remember for years. She claims it can cure homesickness — and nobody has proven her wrong yet. The secret is in the proportion of dried mango to chili.
products.usage: For Papad ki Sabzi, sprinkle on roasted papads, or use as a tangy seasoning for any quick snack.

products.name: Tau Sa's Biryani Masala
products.story: Tau Sa spent 20 years perfecting this blend, travelling from Lucknow to Hyderabad studying biryani masters. His version is uniquely Rajasthani — bolder, more aromatic, with a warmth that lingers. He insists on whole spices being cracked, never ground, for the 'dum' layer.
products.usage: For any biryani — chicken, mutton, or veg. Also elevates pulaos, tehri, and dum-style rice dishes.

comboPacks.name: The Royal Feast Box
comboPacks.description: The ultimate Rajasthani non-veg feast. Everything you need for a royal dinner that'll have guests bowing.

comboPacks.name: The Vegetarian Throne
comboPacks.description: Pure veg, pure royal. Dal Baati to Gatte — the complete Rajasthani vegetarian experience in one box.

comboPacks.name: The Daily Essentials
comboPacks.description: Your everyday kitchen heroes. Chai, Achaar, Biryani — the masalas that make ordinary meals extraordinary.

comboPacks.name: The Street King Box
comboPacks.description: For those who believe the best food comes from the streets. Mirchi Vada + Chai = pure happiness.
